comment: Same subject matter (spatial extent) but a genuine top-level classification divergence: BFO subsumes it under Continuant (immaterial entity); DOLCE files it as an abstract quality-space region, neither endurant nor perdurant. Not `close-match` because the classification difference is structural.

Both are continuant/physical-side categories (BFO: continuant; SUMO: Physical/Object), closer to each other than either is to their own source's Time-side category — but "immaterial entity" and "topographic location under Object" are still distinct formal framings.

BFO=continuant; GFO explicitly declares Space sui generis and disjoint from both Presential and Occurrent (i.e., neither continuant- nor occurrent-equivalent) — a similar non-occurrent classification but via a different top-level architecture.

Both are independent, non-occurrent, region-denoting categories with near-identical naming and near-identical mereological-sum framing ("regions... mereological sums" in GFO; region-of-a-metrics framing in DOLCE) — the closest structural pairing found in this crosswalk, mirroring `time.md`'s equivalent DOLCE/GFO Time pairing.

**Downgraded from `close-match` (2026-07-04 review pass)** — both denote an ordinary spatial/geographic extent, but DOLCE's is an abstract quality-space region while SUMO's is a physical `Object` subtype — an abstract-vs-physical divergence, a stronger disagreement than differing scope/axioms within a shared formal kind (contrast `mapping-004`, where both sides are region-denoting categories on the same side of that divide). `close-match` per `docs/methodology/crosswalk-methodology.md` is reserved for concepts that align for many purposes despite differing scope/axioms/usage; an abstract/physical split is closer to `overlap` ("partially intersect but neither subsumes the other").

**Downgraded from `close-match` (2026-07-04 review pass, for consistency with `time.md`'s equivalent GFO/GUM downgrade)** — both are decomposable, non-atomic spatial-region categories under a broader Space branch, with a parallel atomic counterpart (`Topoid`/`SpacePoint`), but GFO's grounding is metaphysical (Brentano) while GUM's is linguistic (Halliday & Matthiessen) — a difference in the *kind* of independence claimed, not just scope or axioms within a shared formal kind.

TUpper's `spatial_region` is a single undifferentiated mereotopology predicate, not dimensionally subdivided the way BFO's four classes are — a rough correspondence only for the point/zero-dimensional case, not the dimensional apparatus as a whole.

Both function as the general spatial-extent/location node in their respective sources, but SUMO's is a topographic-location Object subtype while GUM's is a clause-level directional-relation range class grounded in systemic-functional linguistics — related in function, not close enough in formal apparatus for `close-match`.

No UFO correspondence identified; zero occurrences of any spatial vocabulary in the paper's full extracted text or the OntoUML vocabulary fallback. Recorded explicitly per this repository's established practice for negative findings.

No reified YAMATO correspondence identified in the one fetchable YAMATO document; scoped absence, not a settled claim — see `note-003` for the more load-bearing informal role Space plays there relative to Time.
